prompts.Q01-main: In one sentence, what does your thesis do?
prompts.Q01-A: Can you make that more specific in terms of the object, method, and result?
prompts.Q01-A1: So where exactly is the innovation?
prompts.Q01-B: Is that one sentence fully consistent with your research objectives?
prompts.Q01-B1: If you could keep only one result, which one would you keep?
prompts.Q02-main: Why did you choose this topic?
prompts.Q02-A: There are many real-world problems. Why is this one worth a PhD study?
prompts.Q02-A1: So are you filling a true blank, or improving work that already exists?
prompts.Q02-B: Why not focus only on spatial design, or only on health services?
prompts.Q02-B1: Then how did you keep the research scope under control?
prompts.Q03-main: Your title is about community space. Why does your framework include many things that are not pure spatial design?
prompts.Q03-A: Are you turning space into everything in the community?
prompts.Q03-B1: Then why not simply call it community environment evaluation?
prompts.Q04-main: How would you summarize your method in one sentence, and how is it different from traditional Delphi and AHP studies?
prompts.Q04-A: You cannot just say integration. What is actually new here?
prompts.Q04-A1: So are you saying you removed subjectivity completely?
prompts.Q04-B: Why is this not just a simple stack of methods?
prompts.Q04-B1: What would happen if you removed one step?
prompts.Q05-main: What is the theoretical foundation of your thesis?
prompts.Q05-A: Which one is the main theory? Do not name everything.
prompts.Q05-A1: Then is the WHO framework a theory or a tool?
prompts.Q05-B: Does your theory only stay in the literature review, or does it really work in the thesis?
prompts.Q05-B1: Did your results also refine the theory in any way?
prompts.Q06-main: How do your research questions, objectives, and final results match each other, and did you achieve them all?
prompts.Q06-A: Can you give one concrete example of that matching logic?
prompts.Q06-A1: Was there any objective that was achieved more weakly than the others?
prompts.Q06-B: Your question is about community space quality, but your result became a full evaluation system. Has the level changed?
prompts.Q06-B1: So is this more of a method study or an applied study?
prompts.Q07-main: How did you select the 410 texts, and what were the selection criteria?
prompts.Q07-A: Why did you not use only academic papers? Why did you also include reports and standards?
prompts.Q07-A1: Among these three sources, which one matters most?
prompts.Q07-B: Could someone else reproduce the same 410 texts by following your rules?
prompts.Q07-B1: Then why did you not publish the full list?
prompts.Q08-A: Would ordinary BERT or manual reading of the literature not be enough?
prompts.Q08-A1: Did you do any comparison to validate that choice?
prompts.Q08-B: Did you choose SciBERT only because it sounds advanced?
prompts.Q08-B1: If you did not use SciBERT, could this study still be done?
prompts.Q09-main: How did you reduce 847 indicators to 55, and what method did you use?
prompts.Q09-A: What were the three most critical filtering steps?
prompts.Q09-A1: Which step was most likely to introduce subjectivity?
prompts.Q09-B: How do you know you did not remove an important indicator by mistake?
prompts.Q09-B1: Was there any indicator you removed and later felt was still important?
prompts.Q10-main: Why did you choose this clustering method?
prompts.Q10-A: K-means is more common. Why did you not use it?
prompts.Q10-A1: Then how do you show that hierarchical clustering worked better here?
prompts.Q10-B: Are these clusters only produced by the algorithm, or do they have real meaning?
prompts.Q10-B1: What if the experts did not agree with the algorithmic grouping?
prompts.Q11-main: How did you validate the expert data in your study?
prompts.Q11-A: Why are those statistics enough to show that the expert data are trustworthy?
prompts.Q11-A1: Was there one indicator that mattered most?
prompts.Q11-B: If the experts came from similar backgrounds, could that create bias?
prompts.Q11-B1: Did you do any bias check for that?
prompts.Q12-main: When you applied the evaluation system to communities, how did you measure indicators such as services and satisfaction?
prompts.Q12-A: Is a satisfaction indicator too soft to be included in the system?
prompts.Q12-A1: Then how did you control its subjective bias?
prompts.Q12-B: How can satisfaction, coverage, and response time be placed in the same system?
prompts.Q12-B1: Does standardization hide the original differences?
prompts.Q13-main: How many experts evaluated each community, and what is the basis for this evaluation method?
prompts.Q13-A: Exactly how many people were involved? Do not just say multidisciplinary.
prompts.Q13-A1: If the number is small, why do you still think the result is reliable?
prompts.Q13-B: Why is this kind of case evaluation method reasonable?
prompts.Q13-B1: If you changed the evaluation team, would the result change?
prompts.Q14-main: How were the data from your three communities obtained?
prompts.Q14-A: Did those data come only from managers, or was there also a resident perspective?
prompts.Q14-A1: Was the share of resident data high?
prompts.Q14-B: Was the source structure of the three communities consistent?
prompts.Q14-B1: If one community had less complete data, would that put it at a disadvantage?
prompts.Q15-main: Do you think three community samples are enough?
prompts.Q15-A: Why do you think these three are representative?
prompts.Q15-A1: So do they represent only Zhengzhou rather than Henan as a whole?
prompts.Q15-B: Why did you not include more communities?
prompts.Q15-B1: Will you expand the sample in the future?
prompts.Q16-main: After evaluating the three communities, what exactly does that show, what was the purpose, and what was the result?
prompts.Q16-A: When you say what it shows, does it support the theory, or does it mainly show the tool is useful?
prompts.Q16-A1: What if the case scores had been very close to each other?
prompts.Q16-B: Was there anything in the results that was different from what you expected?
prompts.Q16-B1: Could that surprise mean your system gives too much weight to social indicators?
prompts.Q17-main: What is the core result of your thesis, and which table or figure best shows it?
prompts.Q17-A: If you had to choose only one, which one would you choose?
prompts.Q17-A1: Then are the top-level weights no longer important?
prompts.Q17-B: If you had to explain the whole thesis with one figure, which one would you choose?
prompts.Q17-B1: Why not choose the method flowchart?
prompts.Q18-A: Everyone knows health and emergency response are important. Why is this still a finding?
prompts.Q18-A1: So is this a theoretical finding or an empirical one?
prompts.Q18-B: You say there are different development paths. How reliable is that conclusion?
prompts.Q18-B1: Could that simply be a coincidence from only three cases?
prompts.Q19-main: Was there anything unusual or especially noteworthy in your study?
prompts.Q19-A: What you mentioned sounds like a finding, not an anomaly. Was there any real anomaly?
prompts.Q19-A1: If some of those definitions were not fully consistent, would that affect your conclusion?
prompts.Q19-B: How did those patterns influence your later discussion?
prompts.Q19-B1: What do they imply for policy?
prompts.Q20-main: What are the limitations of your thesis?
prompts.Q20-A: If you had to name only one, what is the most critical limitation?
prompts.Q20-A1: Would that limitation affect whether the thesis still stands?
prompts.Q20-B: That sounds like a general answer. What was the real difficulty in your study?
prompts.Q20-B1: Did you solve that difficulty completely in the end?
prompts.Q21-main: Does your evaluation system have any generalizability?
prompts.Q21-A: If the weights must be changed, how can you still claim generalizability?
prompts.Q21-A1: Then what is the most stable part of the system?
prompts.Q21-B: Can your framework be used outside China?
prompts.Q21-B1: Then what is the relationship between your system and the WHO framework?
prompts.Q22-main: What is the contribution of your whole evaluation system, and what are the theoretical and practical contributions separately?
prompts.Q22-A: This sounds more like a practical tool. Where is the theoretical contribution exactly?
prompts.Q22-A1: Can that theoretical contribution be used by other researchers?
prompts.Q22-B: Who would actually use your system in practice?
prompts.Q22-B1: Can it be used directly for scoring?
prompts.Q23-main: How can your evaluation system develop in the future?
prompts.Q23-A: If you could choose only one next step, which one matters most?
prompts.Q23-A1: Why not build the digital platform first?
prompts.Q23-B: If you continue this work in a postdoc or a next paper, what would you do first?
prompts.Q23-B1: Would that also change the method?
prompts.Q24-main: What are the future application directions of your thesis, in practical terms, and is there room for expansion?
prompts.Q24-A: Do not give many examples. What is the most realistic application scenario?
prompts.Q24-A1: Then how is it different from an ordinary scoring sheet?
prompts.Q24-B: When you say it can be expanded elsewhere, is that only a vision?
prompts.Q24-B1: If you do extend it, what is the first thing you would need to change?
prompts.Q25-main: Who can use this evaluation system?
prompts.Q25-A: So many groups can use it, but how can they all use the same system?
prompts.Q25-A1: Can residents really use it as well?
prompts.Q25-B: Among all those users, who do you most hope will use it?
prompts.Q25-B1: Then what is the role of researchers?
prompts.Q26-main: Why did you use Fuzzy Delphi, how exactly did it work, and why did you choose these 15 experts?
prompts.Q26-A1: Why did you need the same group of experts in both rounds?
prompts.Q26-B: What exactly were your criteria for selecting the experts?
prompts.Q26-B1: If the experts had very similar backgrounds, could that create bias?
prompts.Q27-main: Why did your Delphi panel not directly include elderly residents?
prompts.Q27-A: If older people were not included, how can you show this is not only a manager's view?
prompts.Q27-A1: Why did you not do a mixed Delphi with experts and residents together?
prompts.Q27-B: You said some experts were close to old age. Can that count as an elderly voice?
prompts.Q27-B1: Then where exactly is the role of elderly residents reflected in the study?
prompts.Q28-main: How did you implement AHP, and how did you ensure consistency and credibility?
prompts.Q28-A: What did you do if an expert's judgments were inconsistent?
prompts.Q28-A1: Why is an average CR of 0.0567 enough to show the result is reliable?
prompts.Q28-B: How did you combine the AHP results from the 15 experts?
prompts.Q28-B1: AHP is subjective by nature. How did you control that subjectivity?
prompts.Q29-main: Why did you finally use AHP instead of machine learning, entropy weighting, or another weighting method?
prompts.Q29-A: You kept stressing objectivity, so why did you still return to AHP in the end?
prompts.Q29-A1: So can we say you were forced to use AHP?
prompts.Q29-B: At what point in the future would you consider replacing AHP with machine learning?
prompts.Q29-B1: So is this one of the biggest limitations of your thesis?
prompts.Q30-main: How did you actually measure the indicators in the three communities and keep the results objective?
prompts.Q30-A: How exactly did you measure soft indicators such as service quality and satisfaction?
prompts.Q30-A1: How can spatial indicators, service indicators, and satisfaction indicators be put into one system?
prompts.Q30-B: You did much of the fieldwork yourself. How can you show the result is fair?
prompts.Q30-B1: If someone else followed your protocol, would they get the same result?
prompts.Q31-main: How did you use PRISMA and data cleaning, and what role did they play in your method chain?
prompts.Q31-A: This looks like technical cleaning. Why do you say it was so important?
prompts.Q31-A1: Then why was the reduction from 847 to 55 not just an arbitrary cut?
prompts.Q31-B: How could someone else reproduce your PRISMA and data cleaning process?
prompts.Q31-B1: What follow-up question is a viva examiner most likely to ask about this part?
